sections.paragraph:
ἁρπαγμός, οῦ, ὁ [ἁρπάζω] in NT only Phil 2:6 as

—1. ‘act of seizing’, robbery, the standard usage in Gk. lit., but considered contextually improbable, hence the preference for

—2. passive sense ‘thing grasped’ (a mng. ordinarily attached to ἅρπαγμα), as a piece of good fortune that one clings to (treasured) windfall, prize, bonanza.
